{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Owen's Defence| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|= |pd| |pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|= | |pd| | | | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= | | | |pl|pl| | | |= | | | | | | | | |= |pl|pl|pl| | |pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= |moves=1.e4 b6 2. d4 |eco=[[Chess/ECOB|B00]] |parent=[[Chess/King's Pawn Opening|King's Pawn Opening]] }} = Owen's Defence = ===2. d4 === A logical developing move. Black almost always responds with 2...Bb7, completing the fianchetto and putting pressure on e4. The alternative [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...b6/2. d4/2...Ba6|2...Ba6]], known as the Guatemala defence, is considered weak as white emerges with an advantage after 3.Bxa6 Nxa6. ==Theory table== {{ChessTable}} '''1.e4 b6 2. d4'''
2 3 4
...
[[/2...Bb7|Bb7]]		 Bd3
Nf6		 Qe2
e6		 +/=
